changes: Major policy shift: WH says Hormuz reopening 'not a core objective' of Iran war (Time/CNN). Iran publishes bridge target list (8 Gulf bridges as 'legitimate targets') after B1 destruction. Pentagon claims 90% of Iran ballistic/drone capability degraded. UN Hormuz vote postponed past Good Friday — no new date; China/Russia oppose. Brent corrected to ~$106 (from ~$108-112). Iran casualties updated to >2,000 killed. Kpler crossings updated to 218 (from 214). Iraq Syria route: SOMO agreed 50k bpd crude + 650k mt/month fuel oil Apr-Jun. ADCOP spare capacity: 71% utilization, can surge to 1.8M bpd. India bought 30M bbl Russian crude in one week. All isNew flags updated for >48hr recency. No new FM declarations or shutdowns found.
newEvents: White House: Hormuz reopening 'not a core objective' of Iran war — 3rd time Trump signaled war may end with strait closed (Time, CNN)

Iran publishes bridge target list: Sheikh Jaber (Kuwait), Sheikh Zayed/Al Maqta/Khalifa (UAE), King Fahd Causeway (Saudi-Bahrain), Jordan bridges, Arik Bridge (Israel) — declared 'legitimate targets' (Fars News, Fox News, Anadolu Agency)
Pentagon claims 90% of Iran's ballistic missile and drone capabilities degraded (NBC News)
UN Hormuz vote postponed past Good Friday — no new date set; China/Russia oppose force authorization (Al-Monitor, Washington Post)
Brent closed $106.04 on Apr 2 (up 4.8%); WTI $104.29 (Business Standard, CNBC)
Iran casualties now >2,000 killed (up from >1,900); 4,700 security forces killed per Iran International (Al Jazeera, Wikipedia)
Kpler: 218 crossings since Mar 1 (up from 214; 94% decline from peacetime) (Kpler, UANI)
Iraq SOMO: 50k bpd crude + 650k mt/month fuel oil via Syria overland to Mediterranean Apr-Jun (The National, Middle East Eye)
ADCOP at 71% utilization, can temporarily surge to 1.8M bpd (Argusmedia, CNBC)
India bought ~30M bbl Russian crude in under a week after US sanctions waiver (Bloomberg, Moscow Times)
noNewFmReason: No new force majeure declarations found since Formosa Plastics Corp (fm-027, Apr 1). Industry warns April will see cascade of FM declarations as naphtha inventories deplete (Lotte Chemical official, C&EN). Monitoring continues.

tier1: Kpler (218 crossings data; cargo flow tracking)
Bloomberg (oil prices, Iran bridge strikes, India-Russia crude)
tier2: Al Jazeera (Iran bridge retaliation, UN vote, war live blogs)

Time (WH reopening Hormuz not vital, Trump Iran off-ramp)
Washington Post (UN vote postponed, markets shudder)
Fox News (Iran bridge target list, B1 collapse)
CBS News (Pentagon 90% degraded, Iran war updates)
NBC News (Iran drone/missile data, Trump primetime speech)
The National UAE (Iraq Syria exports, Bahrain resolution)
Gulf News (Kuwait attacks, Al Salmi tanker)
tier3: UANI (218 Kpler crossings, April 2 shipping update)
The War Zone (B1 bridge destruction details)
Middle East Eye (Iraq Syria land route)
